rulings.Case Title: LISA ARNOLD VS. CLARA ANI BISHOP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Resp To Fcas Frogs To C. Bishop, Set One
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 14, 2026, line 1. DEFENDANT FCA, US, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FCA US LLC'S FORM INTERROGATORIES TO DEFENDANT CLARA ANI BISHOP, SET ONE. Hearing required. The court does not understand how, if counsel for Ms. Bishop has had no contact with Ms. Bishop since the inception of this case, counsel had the authority to file an answer on Ms. Bishop's behalf. The court further wonders whether that answer should be stricken on the court's own motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JUNHAI BAI ET AL VS. STEPHANIE YIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Timely Amend
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 14, 2026, line 2. DEFENDANTS STEPHANIE YIP, SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Timely Amend. Defendants San Francisco Unified School District and Stephanie Yip's motion to dismiss is continued to March 4, 2026 to give plaintiffs Junhai Bai and Xiaofei Li one more opportunity to file a second amended complaint. Absent good cause for their failure to do so, if plaintiffs do not file a second amended complaint by March 3, 2026, at the continued hearing the court will exercise its discretion to dismiss this case per CCP 581(f)(2) due to the failure to file an amended complaint after a demurrer was sustained with leave to amend. Based on the court's review of the document entitled "Reply About Order Sustaining Defendants San Francisco Unified School District and Stephanie Yip's Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint" filed on December 5, 2025, it appears to be plaintiffs' position that they are not required to file a second amended complaint to be able to prevail in this lawsuit. Plaintiffs are mistaken. The effect of the order sustaining the demurrer to the first amended complaint with leave to amend filed September 17, 2025 is that plaintiffs do not have any operative complaint in this case unless and until they file a second amended complaint. Without an operative complaint, the plaintiffs are not able to prevail in this case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE, N.A. VS. DAVIN T GAOIRAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories (Disc-004) And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 14, 2026, line 10. DEFENDANT DAVIN GAOIRAN'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories (Disc-004) And For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant Davin Gaorin's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories and for monetary sanctions is denied without prejudice to filing a further motion contending that the discovery responses served by plaintiff Capital One, N.A. are inadequate. The court credits plaintiff's contention that it didn't receive or inadvertently misplaced the form interrogatories till this motion was filed. Plaintiff has now served responses to the form interrogatories. If defendant believes that those responses are inadequate, he should initiate a meet and confer and, if unsuccessful, he may file a motion seeking further responses. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission (Set One) Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 14, 2026, line 10. DEFENDANT DAVIN GAOIRAN'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission (Set One) Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant Davin Gaorin's motion to deem requests for admission admitted and for monetary sanctions is denied without prejudice to filing a further motion contending that the discovery responses served by plaintiff Capital One, N.A. are inadequate. The court credits plaintiff's contention that it didn't receive or inadvertently misplaced the requests for admission till this motion was filed. Plaintiff has now served responses to the requests for admission. If defendant believes that those responses are inadequate, he should initiate a meet and confer and, if unsuccessful, he may file a motion seeking further responses. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)
